name: nesweep
description: Next Edit Autocomplete using Sweep Model

sweep.enabled.markdownDescription: Enable or disable NESweep suggestions for this window.

sweep.maxContextFiles.markdownDescription: Maximum number of recently-viewed files to bundle into the prompt as additional context (open editors + LSP definitions / usages). Higher values give the model more to work with at the cost of a longer prompt and slower first-token latency.

sweep.autocompleteExclusionPatterns.markdownDescription: File globs / suffixes that NESweep should never request completions for. Defaults cover the common secret-bearing files (`.env`, `.pem`, `.key`, …) so request bodies don't leak credentials to the completion server. Add project-specific patterns to extend.

sweep.autocompleteSnoozeUntil.markdownDescription: Pause completions until the given Unix timestamp (milliseconds). `0` disables the snooze. Set via the `NESweep: Snooze` command rather than editing this directly.

sweep.serverUrl.markdownDescription: Base URL of the OpenAI-compatible `/v1/completions` server serving the model — e.g. llama.cpp's `llama-server`, vLLM, sglang, Ollama with the OpenAI shim. The extension only POSTs to `/v1/completions`; nothing else on the server is touched.

sweep.modelName.markdownDescription:
Model name sent in the request body's `model` field. The prompt format is auto-selected by substring match against this name:

- **Zeta-2.1 (multi-region)** — names containing `zeta-2.1`, `zeta2.1`, `zeta-2-1`, or `zeta_2_1`. Uses paired numbered markers `<|marker_1|>` / `<|marker_2|>` and supports up to 3 editable regions per request (cursor area + nearby diagnostics).
- **Zeta-2 (single-region SeedCoder FIM)** — names containing `zeta-2`, `zeta2`, or `seedcoder`. Uses the legacy git-conflict scaffold `<<<<<<< CURRENT` / `=======` / `>>>>>>> UPDATED`.
- **Sweep `<|file_sep|>` layout** — anything else (including the default `sweepai/sweep-next-edit`). Uses the broad-context + retrieval + `original/current/updated` triplet structure ported from cursortab.nvim.

sweep.completionTimeoutMs.markdownDescription: Per-request timeout in milliseconds. Requests that exceed this are aborted and the suggestion is dropped. Bump it for large prompts on slow hardware; lower it to fail fast on a misconfigured server.

sweep.diagRadius.markdownDescription:
Drop LSP diagnostics whose line is more than this many lines from the cursor before they reach the prompt.

- `0` disables the filter (every diagnostic on the file is kept, capped at 15 entries)
- Default `12` matches cursortab's behaviour and keeps the diagnostics block compact.

sweep.broadBefore.markdownDescription: Lines of broad file context to keep **before** the cursor in the leading `<|file_sep|>{path}` section. Cursortab hardcodes ±150; the asymmetric default (125 before / 75 after) biases toward already-written code, where the model usually needs more context.

sweep.broadAfter.markdownDescription: Lines of broad file context to keep **after** the cursor in the leading `<|file_sep|>{path}` section. See `sweep.broadBefore` for the rationale behind the asymmetric default.

sweep.rulesMaxChars.markdownDescription:
Soft cap on the size of per-language instructions files (`.vscode/nes-<languageId>.md`).

Rules sit at the **front** of every completion prompt — a prefix-cache-friendly slot, so a server with prompt caching (llama.cpp with `-cpent 256` to checkpoint every 256 tokens, vLLM/sglang prefix caching on by default) only re-evaluates the rules block on the first request or when the file changes. The cost of a fat rules file is therefore:

- **Context budget** consumed on every request, even when cached.
- **Prompt-eval latency spike** on first request and after every save.
- **No caching benefit at all** if your server has caching off or the threshold is set above the rules-block size.

Overflow surfaces as a Warning diagnostic + red background on the over-limit region in the editor; it does **not** truncate the file or block completions. Set to `0` to disable the warning.

Default `3000` ≈ ~1000 tokens on the Sweep tokenizer — a reasonable budget for session-stable guidance.

sweep.injectInlineDiagnostics.markdownDescription:
**Mega-hack: inline `// BUG:` comments next to every nearby diagnosed line in the prompt only.**

Appends `<commentPrefix> <marker> (code: <code>) - <message>` to each diagnosed line in the rendered prompt — inline placement next to the affected token gets the model to attend to LSP errors much more reliably than a sibling diagnostics section. The fake comments are stripped from the response before applying, anchored on the literal `<commentPrefix> <marker>` substring so the strip survives the model paraphrasing the rest of the message body.

**When to enable:**
- ✅ **0.5B / 1.5B SweepAI checkpoints** — they routinely ignore the structured diagnostics section and need the inline nudge.
- ❌ **7B SweepAI (default) and 8B Zeta-2 / Zeta-2.1 SeedCoder** — strong enough to pick up the structured block; the inline noise just bloats the prompt.

When on, the structured `context/diagnostics` section is suppressed so the same data isn't emitted twice.

sweep.inlineDiagnosticsMarker.markdownDescription:
Marker phrase emitted between the language's comment prefix and the diagnostic body when `sweep.injectInlineDiagnostics` is on.

For C/C++ the default produces:
```
// BUG: LSP error here (code: undeclared_var_use_suggest) - use 'spdlog' instead (Use of undeclared identifier 'psdlog')
```

Must be a phrase a human would essentially never type — the response-side strip pinpoints our injections by matching the literal `<commentPrefix> <marker>` substring, so colliding with user-authored `TODO:` / `FIXME:` comments is undesirable. Different small models seem to attend to different phrasings; tune per model if the default doesn't get traction.

default.^Implicit conversion loses integer preci: narrow $1 to $2 explicitly with a cast

sweep.diagnosticsMessageTransforms.markdownDescription:
Additional regex transforms applied to every diagnostic message **after** the built-in normalisations.

Built-ins (always active, can't be turned off):
- Strip `(fix available)` / `(fixes available)` suffix
- Rewrite `did you mean 'X'?` → `use 'X' instead`
- Rewrite `consider using 'X'` → `use 'X' instead`
- Rewrite `replace with 'X'` → `use 'X' instead`

User entries:
- **Key** is a JavaScript regex source. Use `$1`, `$2`, … to reference capture groups in the replacement.
- **Value** is the replacement string.
- **Flags** are hardcoded to `i` (case-insensitive). For other flags, encode them in the pattern itself.
- Invalid regexes are silently skipped — a typo in one entry won't break the rest.

Applied uniformly to **both** the structured `context/diagnostics` section and the inline `// BUG:` injection. Defaults cover common clang / clang-tidy patterns; add entries for other linters as needed.
